title: China Sees a ‘Giant With a Limp’ as U.S. Drains Weapons on Iran War
summary: America’s ability to deter China in a war over Taiwan is weakened, Chinese analysts say, giving Beijing leverage in an upcoming summit with President Trump.

title: Trump Reversed Hormuz Plan After Saudis Denied Airspace Access
summary: Saudi Arabia’s refusal of support suggests that President Trump’s unpredictable approach to Iran has strained ties with one of his closest allies in the Middle East.

title: How Energy Prices Are Driving Demand for Solar Panels and Heat Pumps
summary: Already having faced an energy crisis five years ago, more European consumers believe they are “one Trump-ignited war away” from crushing costs.

title: Trade Court Rules Trump’s 10% Global Tariff Is Illegal
summary: A panel of federal judges found that President Trump could not legally impose the tariff on most imports.

title: U.S. Debt Hits a Concerning Milestone, and Experts Say Trump’s Policies Could Worsen It
summary: The debt is outgrowing the size of America’s economy. The president’s policies could accelerate the country’s fiscal headaches, experts say, unless policymakers intervene.

title: Why Stocks and Bonds Are Responding Differently to the Iran War
summary: Stock investors are betting that companies will make enormous profits, despite the war. But investors in bonds, including U.S. Treasuries, have other concerns.

title: U.S. to Review Mexican Consulates After Right-Wing Claims Against Them
summary: The State Department said it would review all Mexican consulates in the United States, following claims in conservative media that the consulates interfere in politics.

title: Health Officials Race to Track Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak but Predict ‘Limited’ Spread
summary: A Dutch flight attendant and French national were among the latest to be tested in connection with a deadly outbreak on a cruise ship. But an analysis in South Africa showed no mutations in viral samples.

title: Should You Worry About Hantavirus?
summary: Here’s what to know about the virus, how it spreads and the risk to the general public.

title: Hantavirus Response Shows How Trump Cuts Have Compromised U.S. Preparedness
summary: The Trump administration has slashed funding for infectious disease research and has far fewer employees, including disease detectives, to respond to outbreaks.

title: Is the Blake Lively v. Justin Baldoni Legal Fight Over? Not So Fast.
summary: Lawyers for Ms. Lively and Mr. Baldoni have said the two sides settled much of the contentious dispute. But one major disagreement remains.

title: Many Russians in No Mood for Celebration on Kremlin’s Biggest Day of the Year
summary: As the country heads into Victory Day, the economy is stalling, internet restrictions are growing and the Kremlin is fearing Ukrainian attacks.

title: Fetterman Says He Has ‘No Plans’ to Leave the Democratic Party
summary: In an opinion piece in The Washington Post, the Pennsylvania senator wrote that while he was “at odds” with the party’s approach to some issues, his values had not changed.

title: Tennessee Approves New Map Aimed at Flipping the Last Democratic Seat
summary: After a Supreme Court ruling that weakened the Voting Rights Act of 1965, Republicans carved up a majority-Black Memphis seat as the national redistricting wars continue.

title: A Project in Europe Tested Whether Students Could Live Without Smartphones
summary: For three weeks, thousands of children in Europe went without social media or their smartphones, or both. This is the diary of their digital detox.

title: Vote Brings ‘Freeze the Rent’ Closer to Reality in New York
summary: The panel that regulates rents for nearly one million apartments cast its first vote since Mayor Zohran Mamdani took office, approving ranges that included no increases.

title: Canvas Online Learning Platform Disabled After Breach by Hackers
summary: Most users regained access to the platform hours after a hacking group said it had attacked Canvas’s parent company and breached 275 million people’s data.

title: Philip Caputo, Who Wrote Blistering Vietnam War Memoir, Dies at 84
summary: “A Rumor of War,” about his service as a Marine Corps infantry officer and published in 1977, relentlessly detailed “the things men do in war and the things war does to them.”

title: New NTSB Report Into Deadly China Eastern Crash Suggests Struggle in Cockpit
summary: A report by the U.S. National Transportation Safety Board offers new details about the China Eastern Airlines crash in 2022, which killed all 132 people on board.

title: Here’s How Freaked Out You Should Be About the Hantavirus Cruise Ship
summary: An expert weighs in.

title: Is Trump About to Invite In the Biggest Predator in the World?
summary: Chinese automakers do not belong on American soil.

title: Late Night Recaps Rubio’s Peacemaking Visit to Pope Leo
summary: Jimmy Fallon joked that President Trump and Pope Leo are putting Marco Rubio, who is Catholic, “in the middle and seeing which parent he goes to.”

title: Rubio Meets Pope Leo Amid Growing Tensions Between Trump and Vatican
summary: The meeting at the Vatican followed President Trump’s condemnation of Pope Leo XIV for opposing the war in Iran.

title: Jason Meade Found Guilty in Fatal Shooting of Casey Goodson Jr. in Ohio
summary: Jason Meade had been searching for a fugitive when he shot Casey Goodson Jr. in 2020. It is rare for a law enforcement official to be convicted in a killing that occurs on the job.

title: German Tourist Wins Lawsuit After Not Getting a Lounge Chair by the Pool
summary: A tourist won a refund after a court ruled that the hotel’s staff had failed to stop guests from reserving sun beds using towels.

title: After Stumbles, Technology Meant for Self-Driving Cars Finds a Second Act
summary: After a crush of promises in 2016 that autonomous cars would soon rule the road, companies whose tech underpinned them have shifted to other industries, like managing shipyards and city traffic.
